preflight.note: Stamped by `repopact new`; the work item was recorded before implementation began.

acceptance_criteria.text: The operator either clears the GitHub Actions billing lock or approves and provisions an alternative remote CI provider; the selected checkpoint and residual trust assumptions are recorded in a durable decision.

Every default-branch change runs governance validation, unit tests, conformance, derived-artifact checks, and distribution build on at least Linux and Windows, with public logs and no dependency on a developer workstation.
The remote checkpoint is required by repository policy or an equivalently enforceable merge gate, and a deliberately invalid test branch proves the gate rejects drift before merge.

Release documentation continues to distinguish direct PyPI fallback publication from trusted automated publishing and does not claim CI-backed enforcement until a green cross-platform run is publicly verified.
